grounding_source_rows: taxonomy Real Property Eminent Domain/Condemnation/Land Use descriptions
grounding_note: 'The government wants my land' spans: a filed condemnation case (Condemnation), pre-filing negotiation over a taking (Eminent Domain), regulatory restrictions without physical taking (Land Use/Zoning), and utility easement demands. The paperwork stage and taking type are user-held facts.

scenarios.opening_query: The state wants a strip of our front acreage for the highway widening and their offer is insulting.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Eminent Domain || Real Property > Condemnation
scenarios.hidden_fact: They've stopped negotiating — we were served with the condemnation complaint Tuesday, so it's a court case now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Negotiation's over — they served the condemnation complaint Tuesday. It's a court case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a condemnation action has been filed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's filed — it's their 'final offer' letter and an appraisal we dispute; the fight is the negotiation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's filed — it's their 'final offer' and a bad appraisal. We're still in negotiation.
scenarios.vetting_note: Filed-vs-negotiating is the label boundary the taxonomy draws.

scenarios.opening_query: The city's new rules basically make our property worthless and nobody will compensate us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Land Use/Zoning || Real Property > Eminent Domain
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's beyond rules — their stormwater project physically floods our back two acres every winter by design; engineers call it a taking that must be compensated.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's physical — their stormwater project deliberately floods our back two acres every winter. Engineers call that a compensable taking.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a physical invasion supports an inverse-condemnation claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's purely regulatory — a new overlay bans the shop building we planned; we want the ordinance challenged or varied.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Purely regulatory — the new overlay bans our planned shop building.
scenarios.vetting_note: Physical-invasion fact converts a zoning grievance into inverse condemnation.

scenarios.opening_query: A power company says it has the right to run new transmission lines straight across our farm.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Eminent Domain || Real Property > Agriculture/Farm
scenarios.hidden_fact: They already hold a 1962 easement — the real dispute is its scope: whether old wording covers modern towers and access roads through our certified-organic fields.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They have a 1962 easement — the fight is whether that old wording covers modern towers and roads through our organic fields.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an existing easement's scope, not a new taking, is disputed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No easement exists — they're threatening to use utility condemnation power if we don't sign their new one.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No easement exists — they're threatening condemnation if we won't sign one.
scenarios.vetting_note: Existing-instrument fact reroutes from taking defense to farm/easement interpretation.

scenarios.opening_query: The county has been using the edge of our land as a maintenance turnaround for years and now claims it's theirs.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > General (Residential) || Real Property > Litigation || Real Property > Eminent Domain
scenarios.hidden_fact: They've now recorded a claim of prescriptive rights — quiet-title litigation is the response, on a clock.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They recorded a prescriptive-rights claim — we need quiet-title litigation, and there's a clock on it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a recorded adverse claim requires title litigation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's recorded — it's an encroachment we want documented and stopped with a boundary agreement.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing recorded — we want it documented and stopped with a boundary agreement.
scenarios.vetting_note: Recorded-claim fact escalates neighborly-boundary work to title litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: Our church property is under threat from the city's redevelopment plans and the congregation is frightened.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Eminent Domain || General Litigation > Civil Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: The plan spares every secular building on our block and takes only the two churches — counsel we consulted says the religious-targeting pattern is a civil-rights case alongside any taking defense.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The plan spares every secular building on the block and takes only the two churches — that targeting pattern is a civil-rights case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether discriminatory targeting accompanies the taking
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No targeting — the whole block goes for the transit hub; our fight is valuation and relocation costs.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No targeting — the whole block goes. Our fight is valuation and relocation.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pattern-evidence fact adds the constitutional overlay.

scenarios.opening_query: We got a letter about our land being in the path of a public project and we don't know what happens next.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Condemnation || Real Property > Land Use/Zoning || Real Property > Eminent Domain
scenarios.hidden_fact: Reading it closely, nothing is being taken — it's a proposed corridor overlay that would freeze development on our parcel while they study the route; fighting the overlay designation is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Nothing's being taken — it's a corridor overlay freezing development while they study the route. Fighting that designation is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the letter proposes a taking or a regulatory designation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the real thing — a notice of intent to acquire with a deposit of estimated compensation and a court date.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the real thing — notice of intent to acquire, deposited compensation, court date.
scenarios.vetting_note: Letter-content fact; overlay freezes vs actual acquisition.
